rows.definition: The internal/guardrsi package that folds a guarded session's hash-chained decision journal and finds the dominant honesty-hole bucket (WorstBucket) to drive the guard-verdict RSI loop.
rows.distinction: It is the JOURNAL-FOLD measurement layer over a guard's own verdicts, NOT the guard kernel that emits them and NOT the shipgate that promotes a code change.

rows.definition: The internal/guardroute bridge that turns guardrsi's worst journal bucket into a routed, idempotent, escalating finding (a queue row and, for a real honesty-hole, a deduped GitHub issue), closing the guard RSI loop.
rows.distinction: It is the DECISION/ROUTING layer that materializes a fold into a sink, NOT guardrsi (the fold that produces the bucket) and NOT the guard kernel itself.

rows.definition: The internal/guardcomplaint package behind `fak complain`: the SUBJECTIVE appeal channel where an agent reports a false-positive DENY, the complement of guardroute's objective journal-fold routing.
rows.distinction: It is the agent-initiated APPEAL/complaint layer (a felt false positive), NOT guardroute (the automatic objective routing) and NOT the guard kernel's own refusal.

rows.definition: The cmd/guarddemo command: the live on-box demo that replays the same attack trace twice (with and without fak) to show the SAFETY FLOOR refusing destructive/exfiltration tool calls.
rows.distinction: It is a DEMO harness that exhibits the floor's behavior, NOT the guard kernel that enforces it and NOT guardtrace (the hermetic test/replay harness).

rows.definition: The cmd/fak guard Stop-hook half of the deny-all false-stop fix: when the floor refused EVERY tool call in a turn (reported as end_turn), it auto-RESUMES the agent, bounded by warn/final consecutive-continue limits.
rows.distinction: It is a Claude-Code STOP-hook actuator that recovers from a floor-induced false stop, NOT a tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the PreCompact mode hook.

rows.definition: The guard_mcp_status_audit make-hygiene checker (schema fak-guard-mcp-status-audit/1) that audits whether the guard's default MCP blockers are in place and surfaces them in the TUI guard pane.
rows.distinction: It is a STATUS/CONFIG audit of the guard's MCP posture, NOT a runtime adjudication gate and NOT guardtrace (the trace-replay harness).

rows.definition: The cmd/fak TUI data model for the guard pane: tuiGuardReport (built by buildTUIGuardReport from artifacts loaded by loadTUIGuard) holds the counts, rows, and sources rendered as the guard overview card.
rows.distinction: It is the read-only RENDER/REPORT model that visualizes guard journal artifacts in the TUI, NOT the guard kernel that produces them and NOT any adjudication gate.

rows.definition: The cmd/fak entry function for `fak guard`: it resolves the upstream wire, stands up the gateway, builds the child command (buildGuardChild) with the injected base-URL env (guardInjectedEnv), and execs the wrapped agent through the kernel.
rows.distinction: It is the CLI LAUNCHER that wires up a guarded session, NOT the in-process adjudication kernel concept itself and NOT any single decision gate.

rows.definition: The cmd/fak guard provider-resolution family: GuardProvider/resolveGuardProvider/guardDetectProvider pick the upstream API wire (anthropic/openai/...) for a guarded session, inferring it from the wrapped agent's base name (guardAgentBaseName).
rows.distinction: It selects WHICH upstream provider the guard proxies, NOT a tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the base-URL string those providers map to.

rows.definition: The cmd/fak guard base-URL config helpers: guardDefaultBaseURL maps a provider to its public API base, guardOpenAIV1Base appends the /v1 the OpenAI-compatible wire expects, and fleetDogfoodGuardBaseURL is the fleet dogfood default.
rows.distinction: These are the upstream BASE-URL strings the guard proxies to, NOT the provider-resolution logic that picks the provider and NOT an adjudication gate.

rows.definition: The dispatch/guard launch + session config helpers: GuardedLaunchCommand fronts a worker command with `fak guard`, guardEnabled gates whether to do so, guardEnvValue/guardedGlobs/guardLocalModelDecision/guardTimeoutFloors/guardWaitHealthy supply the session's env, scope, model and readiness wiring.
rows.distinction: These are the SESSION-SETUP knobs that decide whether and how a child runs under the guard, NOT the adjudication gates inside it and NOT the provider/base-URL resolvers.

rows.definition: The cmd/fak guardDefaultAuditPath helper: the per-user default filesystem path where `fak guard` writes its durable hash-chained decision journal when FAK_AUDIT_JOURNAL is unset.
rows.distinction: It is the JOURNAL OUTPUT PATH config for the guard, NOT the guard kernel itself and NOT a decision gate.

rows.definition: The off|shadow|enforce mode constants for `fak guard`'s Claude-Code PreCompact / Stop hooks: guardPreCompactModeOff disables, guardPreCompactModeShadow logs the would-block while exiting 0, and guardPreCompactModeEnforce returns the blocking exit code.
rows.distinction: It is the THREE-WAY MODE config for the hook actuators (the compaction/stop posture), NOT a tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the guard kernel.

rows.definition: The cmd/fak installGuardCodexConfig function: the only thing that puts the kernel in front of Codex, rewriting a `codex` child command with Codex-specific `-c` flags so it routes through the guard gateway.
rows.distinction: It is the Codex-only COMMAND REWRITE that wires Codex to the gateway, NOT the generic child builder and NOT an adjudication gate.

rows.definition: The sessionobs.Signals counter that tallies how many times the guard refused a tool call in a session (deny + kernel-deny + per-turn floor refusals), excluding quarantines.
rows.distinction: It is a per-session COUNT of refusals (a measurement), NOT the guard mechanism that refuses and NOT a gate that decides.

rows.definition: The report-level CI gate on cadencereport/dojo/gardenbundle reports: CheckGate(report) returns an exit code plus message, and WithGate folds that gate result (gateExit / gateMessage) back into the emitted report.
rows.distinction: It is a REPORT-SUMMARY CI-pass/fail gate over an already-computed report, NOT a kernel adjudication gate on a tool call and NOT a result admitter.

rows.definition: The internal/a2achan capability-floor functions gateSend (send-time) and gateRecv (recv-time): the one fail-closed source of truth the agent-to-agent Bus and replay call to admit or deny a channel message by capability.
rows.distinction: It is the A2A-MESSAGE capability floor (send/recv on a channel), NOT a tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the report CI gate.

rows.definition: The internal/accounts twin-guard GateTokenWrite: it twin-checks before writing an account's .oauth-token so a credential is never smeared across two distinct accounts in one seat directory.
rows.distinction: It is a CREDENTIAL-WRITE admission check (prevent cross-account token smear), NOT a tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the A2A message floor.

rows.definition: The cachevalueledger cache-value gate accounting: MinGateTurns is the minimum number of multi-turn turns required before the regression gate enforces, while GatePromptTokens / GateReusedTokens are the prompt/reused token tallies that gate folds over.
rows.distinction: These are the THRESHOLD and TOKEN TALLIES for the cache-value regression gate (a metric guard), NOT a tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the report CI gate.

rows.definition: The internal/agent gateTurn method: it applies the per-turn SessionGate at a turn boundary, returning the per-turn token cap, a proceed bool, and a stop reason (and sleeping any throttle gap) — wired by WithSessionGate.
rows.distinction: It is a SESSION-PACING gate at the loop turn boundary (cap/proceed/throttle), NOT a tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the report CI gate.

rows.definition: The internal/hooks staged-diff commit-boundary check functions: gateDocPlacement (doc placement rule), gateProvenanceLabel (provenance labels), and gatePublicLeak (public-leak scrub) each scan a StagedDiff and return findings.
rows.distinction: These are concrete STATIC commit-boundary check functions (the git-hook gate implementations), NOT a runtime adjudication gate and NOT gitgate (the runtime git-command prefilter).

rows.definition: The internal/ifc GatedSinks policy data: the configured set of egress/destructive/exec sink classes the SinkGate adjudicator reads to decide whether tainted arguments may flow into a sink.
rows.distinction: It is the POLICY DATA (the sink set) the SinkGate enforces, NOT the SinkGate adjudicator itself and NOT a model gating weight.

rows.definition: The internal/pythongate leaf: a ratchet (TestNoNewPythonTools) that refuses introducing new Python tool scripts, holding the line on the Go-first tooling preference.
rows.distinction: It is a SOURCE-TREE ratchet preventing new Python tools, NOT a runtime tool-call adjudication gate and NOT a git-hook commit gate.

rows.definition: The internal/loopgate leaf: the STOP gate for the verified loop that adjudicates whether a goal is witnessed-done, returning verdicts like NotYet with reasons (DoneUnwitnessed) over criteria such as commit-audit or citation-resolve.
rows.distinction: It is the LOOP COMPLETION gate (is the goal proven done?), NOT a tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the report CI gate.

rows.definition: The internal/windowgate leaf: the no-desktop-popup gate (TestTrackedTreeHasNoPopups) that refuses code which would raise a blocking desktop window/popup.
rows.distinction: It is a SOURCE-TREE gate against desktop popups, NOT a runtime tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the loop completion gate.

rows.definition: The internal/benchlineagegate leaf: the durable enforcement gate (TestEveryBenchEmitterStampsLineage) that requires every benchmark emitter to stamp its lineage.
rows.distinction: It is a BENCH-EMITTER hygiene gate (lineage stamping), NOT a runtime tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the no-popup windowgate.

rows.definition: The internal/engine residencyGate: the rank-12 engine-residency Adjudicator that DENIES a tenant-scoped tool call routed to a remote/off-device path (ReasonTrustViolation).
rows.distinction: It is a registered PRE-CALL ADJUDICATOR rung (deny remote tenant routing), NOT a result admitter and NOT a source-tree hygiene gate.

rows.definition: The internal/swebench SmokeGate: one named smoke-check row in the opus-smoke harness (rendered by renderSmokeGatesTable as a Gate | OK | Detail markdown table) recording whether a pre-run readiness check passed.
rows.distinction: It is a SMOKE-CHECK readiness gate row in a benchmark harness, NOT a runtime tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the loop completion gate.

rows.definition: Local identifiers in internal/model's weight-loading code: gateUp is the concatenated gate++up tensor in the fused-split path, and gateName is the gate_proj weight's resolved name in the safetensors quantizer.
rows.distinction: These are NEURAL-NET FFN gate-projection LOAD-TIME locals (handling gate_up_proj / gate_proj weights), NOTHING to do with adjudication gates; siblings of mlp.gate_proj, not the guard kernel.

rows.definition: The NewGate constructors in internal/headroom and internal/rehydrate: each builds a fresh admission Gate (independent counters / sorted rungs) used as a post-call admit ladder.
rows.distinction: It is a GATE-OBJECT CONSTRUCTOR for an admission ladder, NOT a specific named gate (StampGate/SinkGate) and NOT the guard kernel.

rows.definition: The internal/gateway newLiftGuard constructor: builds the streaming lift-guard that watches a model's streamed content (via an emit callback) during the planner/proxy stream path.
rows.distinction: It is a STREAM-CONTENT lift guard in the gateway emit path, NOT a tool-call adjudication gate and NOT the guard kernel.

rows.definition: The FleetProcResourceGuard Windows Scheduled Task (register_proc_resource_guard.ps1): the process-resource peer of the find/grep runaway guard that kills runaway fleet processes by resource budget.
rows.distinction: It is an OS-level RESOURCE guardrail killing runaway processes, NOT the in-process guard kernel and NOT a tool-call adjudication gate.

rows.definition: The environment-variable toggles for two adjudication rungs: FAK_GITGATE turns the internal/gitgate git-shape prefilter on/off, and FAK_NORMGATE turns the internal/normgate canonicalize-and-rescan ResultAdmitter on/off.
rows.distinction: These are ENV-VAR ON/OFF SWITCHES for the gitgate and normgate rungs, NOT the rungs themselves and NOT the guard kernel.
